title: ruppia cirrhosa subsp. longipes
intro: Ruppia cirrhosa subsp. longipes, also known as the long-stemmed ruppia, is a subspecies of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Ruppiaceae. The medicinal properties of Ruppia cirrhosa subsp. longipes lie in its ability to act as a diuretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant, which can help to relieve urinary tract issues and reduce inflammation. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, cough, and digestive problems, due to its health benefits of reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to leverage its medicinal properties and health benefits, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
